verifiedBy: figures fetched from the vendors' live pricing pages on 2026-07-10 and independently re-fetched by a second pass the same day; every rate carries its verbatim quote + URL. Vendor page last-updated stamps: Cloudflare Workers pricing 2026-07-07, static-assets billing 2026-04-23; Vercel /docs/pricing 2026-06-16, functions usage-and-pricing 2026-06-16, manage-cdn-usage 2026-06-23, regional-pricing/iad1 2026-02-13.

rates.quote: 10 million included per month +$0.30 per additional million
rates.note: every browser-observed request to the composed origin invokes the front Worker; service-binding sub-requests are not billed extra ("Requests made from your Worker to another worker via a Service Binding do not incur additional request fees.")

rates.quote: 30 million CPU milliseconds included per month +$0.02 per additional million CPU milliseconds
There are no additional charges for data transfer (egress) or throughput (bandwidth).

unmeasured.note: the edge Worker's warm-tier KV reads happen server-side, per data fetch — the browser-observed request count cannot account them

allowances.quote: 10 milliseconds of CPU time per invocation
allowances.note: a per-invocation CAP, not a daily pool — the measured profile carries per-visit totals, so fit cannot be verified from it

freePlan.overflow: beyond the free allowance requests fail rather than bill: "If you exceed your free tier request limits, these requests will receive a 429 (Too Many Requests) response instead of falling back to static asset serving."

caveats: the Workers Paid $5/month is a minimum account charge bundling Workers, Pages Functions, KV, Hyperdrive, and Durable Objects: "The Workers Paid plan includes Workers, Pages Functions, Workers KV, Hyperdrive, and Durable Objects usage for a minimum charge of $5 USD per month for an account."

Cloudflare Workers pricing is flat/global — no regional price table exists on the pricing page (the only region-priced item there is the separate Containers product), so these rates answer for any stated region

rates.quote: Requests to static assets are free and unlimited.
Requests to static assets are free and unlimited.
rates.note: models the pure static-asset serving path, which executes no billable Worker CPU; measured CPU-ms from a Worker-fronted plane does not apply on this host model — requests routed to a Worker instead bill as Workers ("Requests to the Worker script (for example, in the case of SSR content) are billed according to Workers pricing.")

rates.quote: There are no additional charges for data transfer (egress) or throughput (bandwidth).

allowances.quote: Requests to static assets are free and unlimited.

freePlan.overflow: static asset requests do not overflow (free and unlimited); Worker-routed requests on the free plan 429 beyond 100,000/day

caveats: this host models a variant deployed AS pure static assets; "When using run_worker_first, requests matching the specified patterns will always invoke your Worker script." — such requests bill as Workers requests instead

hosts.plan: Pro (fluid compute, on-demand + $20 monthly usage credit)

rates.quote: Edge Requests | Included First 10,000,000, then $2.00 per 1,000,000 Requests
rates.note: every CDN request, static assets included: "Static assets and functions all incur CDN Requests." "CDN Requests appear as Edge Requests in your billing dashboard" (https://vercel.com/docs/manage-cdn-usage); us-east = Vercel iad1 (Washington D.C.), regional range $2.00–$3.20/1M

rates.quote: If your function receives 1.5 million requests on a Pro plan, Vercel bills those invocations at $0.60 per million, subject to your monthly Pro usage credit.
rates.note: stated assumption: one function invocation per visit — the server-rendered document; client-side fetches that hit functions would add invocations this per-visit basis does not count. No regional invocation prices are published; the flat $0.60/1M is applied ("Starting at $0.60 per 1M" on vercel.com/pricing)

rates.quote: | Washington D.C., USA (iad1) | $0.128 | $0.0106 | — 'The prices are per hour for CPU and per GB-hr for memory'; 'Billed per CPU-hour'
rates.note: Active CPU pauses during I/O ("You are only billed during actual code execution and not during I/O operations") — measured CPU-ms, not wall-time, is the matching input; us-east = iad1, regional range $0.128–$0.221/CPU-hr

rates.quote: Fast Data Transfer | Included First 1 TB, then $0.15 per 1 GB
rates.note: billed cached or uncached ("Requests to static assets incur the same Fast Data Transfer usage if the response is cached or uncached."); includedPerMonth is 1 TB expressed in GB; us-east = iad1, regional range $0.15–$0.35/GB

unmeasured.quote: Billed for the entire instance lifetime in GB-hours
unmeasured.note: a second compute dimension billed on wall-time × memory ("Memory is reserved for your function even when it's waiting for I/O") — the measured profile carries CPU-ms only, so pricing only Active CPU undercounts Vercel compute

unmeasured.priceSummary: $0.06/GB at iad1 (regional range $0.06–$0.43), no included allotment in the iad1 table
unmeasured.note: bytes between the CDN and functions, billed on BOTH directions for every function-backed request ("Usage is incurred on both the input and output data transfer when using compute or blob on Vercel.") — the browser-side measured bytes cannot decompose CDN↔function transfer

unmeasured.priceSummary: $0.30/CPU-hr at iad1 (regional range $0.30–$0.48), metered in 10ms increments
unmeasured.quote: Edge Requests - Additional CPU Duration | $0.30 per 1 Hour — 'CDN Requests of 10ms or less in duration don't incur any additional charges. CPU duration is metered in increments of 10ms.'
unmeasured.note: routing-layer CPU per CDN request; not observable from the browser and typically negligible for a simple visit profile

allowances.note: checked on the visits basis under the same one-invocation-per-visit assumption as the Pro rate

allowances.quote: Active CPU | 4 hours included (Hobby column of the fluid compute pricing table)
Provisioned Memory | 360 GB-hrs included (Hobby column of the fluid compute pricing table)
allowances.note: not checkable: the measured profile has no wall-time × memory dimension

freePlan.overflow: Hobby is non-commercial and does not bill overage; exceeding the allowance pauses the deployment or requires upgrading to Pro

caveats: Pro has NO included allotment for invocations or Active CPU (docs table shows N/A) — they bill on demand from the first unit, offset by the plan's monthly credit: Pro is "$20/user/month" with "$20 of included usage credit"; monthlyBaseUsd assumes one seat
region prices are the Vercel iad1 (Washington D.C.) column — the cheapest published region; this card's us-east maps to iad1

this host models a FUNCTION-BACKED (server-rendered) variant — the invocation meter's one-per-visit assumption is false for a static deployment, whose documents come from the CDN with zero invocations; price static variants on vercel-pro-static instead

hosts.plan: Pro (static deployment: CDN only, no functions)

rates.quote: Edge Requests | Included First 10,000,000, then $2.00 per 1,000,000 Requests
rates.note: "Static assets and functions all incur CDN Requests." (https://vercel.com/docs/manage-cdn-usage); us-east = iad1, regional range $2.00–$3.20/1M

rates.quote: Static assets and functions all incur CDN Requests.
rates.note: a static deployment executes no billable function CPU — documents and assets are served from the CDN; measured CPU-ms from a Worker-fronted measurement plane does not apply on this host model

rates.quote: Fast Data Transfer | Included First 1 TB, then $0.15 per 1 GB
rates.note: billed cached or uncached ("Requests to static assets incur the same Fast Data Transfer usage if the response is cached or uncached."); us-east = iad1, regional range $0.15–$0.35/GB

unmeasured.priceSummary: $0.30/CPU-hr at iad1 (regional range $0.30–$0.48), metered in 10ms increments
unmeasured.quote: Edge Requests - Additional CPU Duration | $0.30 per 1 Hour — 'CDN Requests of 10ms or less in duration don't incur any additional charges. CPU duration is metered in increments of 10ms.'
unmeasured.note: routing-layer CPU per CDN request; not observable from the browser and typically negligible for a simple visit profile

freePlan.overflow: Hobby is non-commercial and does not bill overage; exceeding the allowance pauses the deployment or requires upgrading to Pro

caveats: models a variant deployed as pure static output on Vercel (no functions, no ISR): the paradigm difference against vercel-pro — zero invocations, zero Active CPU, zero Fast Origin Transfer — is what this block exists to price honestly

notes: rates are marginal (beyond included monthly allotments); the $/1M-visits views price at marginal rates — included allotments, base fees, and credits enter the actual-charge view

ISR Reads and Runtime Cache Reads ($0.40/1M read units at iad1) are Vercel cache/storage-feature charges incurred only by ISR/Data-Cache-backed pages — out of this card's per-visit scope, noted so the omission is visible
R2 operation pricing was not verified this session — if a host model ever needs R2 reads priced, verify it first (never fill from training data)
